ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF. Both ORIGINAL (page 1) and COPY (page 2) versions present. ORIGINAL used for verification.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF, 4 pages. Invoice No 61622342/1S issued by AB Volvo Penta.

lc_check.lc_validation_details: The certificate contains the text 'LC: ZHALC25148' in the Remarks field (box 5). 'LC:' is a SEPARATE label/field identifier preceding the number 'ZHALC25148'. This constitutes a separate field label for Letter of Credit, not merely letters 'LC' embedded in a reference number. The invoice confirms 'LC NO：ZHALC25148' on page 1. LC exception is activated per section 2.2. Only Country of Origin (4.5) will be verified. All other checkpoints set to NOT_APPLICABLE.

country_of_origin.invoice_value: ORIGIN: FRANCE (page 1 explicit field); per-item origins: FR, SE, LT, PL, DE, CZ, US, AT (pages 2-4)

rules_applied.description: LC exception – only origin verification performed. Origin must be found in an explicit origin field in the invoice.
EU normalization – 'European Community' verified if invoice contains at least one EU member state origin.
EU member state equivalence – cert=EU ↔ invoice=EU member state is permitted in both directions.
Country of origin verification – each origin in certificate must be explicitly identifiable in invoice.
MATCH requires every origin country in the certificate to be explicitly identifiable in the invoice.
Critical rule: Non-EU origins in invoice that are NOT listed in certificate's origin field constitute a MISMATCH.

comparison_steps.details: Certificate box 3 lists two origins: (1) 'European Community' and (2) 'The United States'.

comparison_steps.description: Identify explicit origin fields in invoice
comparison_steps.details: Invoice page 1 contains an explicit field 'ORIGIN: FRANCE'. This is the summary-level origin field. Invoice pages 2-4 contain per-item 'Origin' column with codes: FR (main engine and tank solenoid kit), SE (pre-fuel filter, bracket mounting kit x2, pressure pipe installation kit), LT (extension cable, wiring harness pre-NOx, ACM kit, connector kit), PL (AdBlue tank, wiring harnesses x5, air cleaner, hoses x3, airfilter sensor cable), DE (SCR muffler, DPF muffler), CZ (EATS pipe 4, exhaust clamp kit, air cleaner for AdBlue), US (EATS component kit), AT (pump unit kit).

Certificate states 'European Community'. Invoice contains origins FR, SE, DE, LT, PL, CZ, AT – all EU member states. Per rule 4.5.4.1, 'European Community' is verified when the invoice contains at least one EU member state origin. Multiple EU member states found. 'European Community' is VERIFIED.

Certificate states 'The United States'. Invoice per-item Origin column shows 'US' for item 24686371 (EATS component kit) on page 3. 'US' is the ISO code for The United States. This is an explicit per-item origin field. 'The United States' is VERIFIED.

comparison_steps.description: Check for non-EU origins in invoice not listed in certificate (rule 4.5.4.1 critical rule)
comparison_steps.details: Certificate origin field (box 3) lists: 'European Community' and 'The United States'. Invoice per-item origins include 'US' – this is covered by 'The United States' in the certificate. All other per-item origins are EU member states (FR, SE, DE, LT, PL, CZ, AT) – covered by 'European Community'. No non-EU origin in the invoice is unaccounted for in the certificate's origin field.

comparison_steps.description: LC-specific rule: Check if origins are in explicit origin fields
comparison_steps.details: Per rule 2.2 critical rule, at LC exception, each origin must be identifiable in an EXPLICIT origin field in the invoice. The invoice has: (1) 'ORIGIN: FRANCE' as a dedicated summary origin field on page 1 – this is an EU member state, covered by 'European Community'. (2) Per-item 'Origin' column on pages 2-4 with country codes – these are per-item origin fields at the line level. Rule 2.2 states: 'It is NOT sufficient that the country appears only in: a column marked Origin or Orig at line level without connection to a collective field'. However, re-reading carefully: the rule says per-item Origin columns are NOT sufficient. The explicit collective ORIGIN field on page 1 states 'FRANCE'. France is an EU member state, so 'European Community' is verifiable via this collective field. But 'The United States' does NOT appear in the collective ORIGIN field on page 1 (which says only 'FRANCE'). 'US' only appears in per-item Origin columns. Per the LC critical rule, this is NOT sufficient for LC verification of 'The United States'.
comparison_steps.result: MISMATCH – 'The United States' cannot be identified in an explicit collective origin field; it only appears in per-item line-level origin columns

country_of_origin.motivation: LC exception is active. Certificate lists two origins: 'European Community' and 'The United States'. The invoice's explicit collective origin field (page 1) states 'ORIGIN: FRANCE' – France is an EU member state, so 'European Community' is verified via this field. However, 'The United States' does NOT appear in any explicit collective/dedicated origin field in the invoice. The US origin only appears in the per-item 'Origin' column at line level (item 24686371, origin code 'US'). Per rule 2.2 critical rule, at LC exception, each origin must be identifiable in an EXPLICIT origin field such as 'Country of Origin', 'Origin', 'Made in' – not merely in a per-item column at line level. Since 'The United States' cannot be identified in a dedicated collective origin field, this constitutes MISMATCH.
country_of_origin.debug_explanation: Certificate box 3: 'European Community / The United States'. Invoice page 1 explicit 'ORIGIN: FRANCE' field – France is EU member → 'European Community' verified. 'The United States' is NOT in the collective ORIGIN field. US only appears as 'US' in per-item Origin column (page 3, item 24686371). Per section 2.2 critical rule: 'It is NOT sufficient that the country appears only in: a per-item column marked Origin or Orig at line level without connection to a collective field.' Therefore 'The United States' fails LC-level origin verification. Result: MISMATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Accept US origin from per-item Origin column as sufficient for LC verification
rejected_hypotheses.reason: Rule 2.2 critical rule explicitly states that per-item line-level origin columns are NOT sufficient for LC origin verification. A dedicated/collective origin field is required.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Treat 'ORIGIN: FRANCE' as covering all origins since it's on the summary page
rejected_hypotheses.reason: The field explicitly states 'FRANCE' only, not 'FRANCE / THE UNITED STATES'. This field does not cover US origin.

evidence.section: Dedicated ORIGIN field on invoice page 1

rule_evaluation_log.impact: Only country of origin (4.5) will be verified. All other checkpoints set to NOT_APPLICABLE.

rule_evaluation_log.result: Document A (Certificate of Origin) contains ORIGINAL and COPY certifikate pages. Document B (Invoice) contains 4-page invoice 61622342/1S. ORIGINAL certificate (page 1) used. Invoice from file 2 used for verification.
PASSED – All three criteria met: invoice reference 61622342/1S matches, consignor AB Volvo Penta identified in invoice, consignee SANY MARINE HEAVY INDUSTRY CO., LTD identified in invoice.

rule_evaluation_log.description: EU normalization – European Community verified against EU member states in invoice
rule_evaluation_log.result: VERIFIED – Invoice contains multiple EU member state origins (FR, SE, DE, LT, PL, CZ, AT). European Community is verified.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Verification of 'The United States' in explicit origin field
rule_evaluation_log.result: FAILED – Invoice's collective ORIGIN field on page 1 states 'FRANCE' only. 'The United States' not found in any collective/dedicated origin field.
rule_evaluation_log.impact: Second origin NOT verified at LC level.

rule_evaluation_log.description: LC critical rule – origin must be in explicit origin field, not per-item column
rule_evaluation_log.result: APPLIED – 'US' found only in per-item Origin column at line level, not in collective/dedicated origin field. Per LC critical rule, this is insufficient.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Certifikatet anger två ursprungsländer: 'European Community' och 'The United States'. Fakturans samlade ursprungsfält anger enbart 'ORIGIN: FRANCE'. Medan 'European Community' kan verifieras via Frankrike (EU-medlemsstat), saknas 'The United States' i fakturans dedikerade ursprungsfält – US-ursprung framgår bara på artikelradnivå. Eftersom ett Letter of Credit är aktivt krävs att varje ursprungsland återfinns i ett dedikerat ursprungsfält, vilket inte är uppfyllt för USA.
summary_of_mismatches: Country of Origin: Certificate lists 'The United States' as one of two origins. The invoice's collective ORIGIN field states only 'FRANCE'. US origin appears only in per-item line-level Origin column, which is insufficient under LC exception rules.
blocking_issues: Origin 'The United States' not identifiable in a dedicated collective origin field in the invoice (only in per-item column).

sections.invoice_value: ORIGIN: FRANCE (collective field, page 1); per-item origins include US (line level only)
sections.rule_applied: LC exception origin verification (section 2.2) requires each origin to be in an explicit dedicated origin field. EU normalization (4.5.4.1) applied for 'European Community'. 'The United States' not found in any collective origin field – only in per-item column at line level, which is insufficient under LC rules.
sections.motivation: Certificate lists two origins: 'European Community' and 'The United States'. The invoice's collective ORIGIN field on page 1 states 'FRANCE'. France is an EU member state, verifying 'European Community'. However, 'The United States' is absent from the collective ORIGIN field; it appears only as 'US' in the per-item Origin column for one component (EATS component kit). Under LC exception rules, per-item line-level origin columns are not sufficient – a dedicated collective origin field is required. Therefore 'The United States' cannot be verified, resulting in MISMATCH.

human_readable_report.final_result: NOT_IDENTICAL – Under the LC exception, only Country of Origin was verified. The certificate's origin 'The United States' could not be identified in a dedicated collective origin field in the invoice. Manual handling required.

output_quality_checks.notes: One applicable checkpoint (country_of_origin) has status MISMATCH → comparison_result = NOT_IDENTICAL. Workflow = MANUAL_HANDLING_REQUIRED. All non-origin checkpoints are NOT_APPLICABLE due to LC exception. Consistency verified.
